_note: Demo-fixtures for the hero projects. VCS IDs verified against the Verra registry API (https://registry.verra.org/uiapi/resource/resourceSummary/{id}). 'bbox' is [minx, miny, maxx, maxy] in WGS84; used only when no real polygon is registered. KML/GeoJSON paths under data/raw/verra/ are real boundaries from each project's PDD or WDPA.

projects.name: Cikel Brazilian Amazon REDD APD — Avoiding Planned Deforestation
projects.scandal_notes: Featured in The Guardian (Jan 2023) investigation reporting that 90%+ of Verra rainforest credits were 'phantom'. Cikel-Brasil Verde S.A. timber concession in Paragominas, Pará. Microsoft is named in the investigation as a buyer; specific retirement volumes are not publicly disclosed in the Verra retirement registry.

projects.name: Pacajai REDD+ Project (formerly ADPML Portel-Pará)
projects.scandal_notes: Suspended by Verra over land-grabbing allegations against ADPML Limited (Guernsey); ~10M credits sold before suspension. Subject of REDD-Monitor and Context investigations. Project area aggregates 15 fazenda landholdings in Portel municipality.

projects.name: Cordillera Azul National Park REDD
projects.scandal_notes: Flagged by CarbonPlan; deforestation baselines disputed in academic literature. Shell named in 2022 Friends of the Earth Netherlands report as buyer of 'most of its carbon credits' from this project. TotalEnergies documented purchases ~$84.7M (Mongabay/AP investigation, 2023). >28M credits issued.

projects.name: Mai Ndombe REDD+ Project
projects.scandal_notes: Wildlife Works project. Salesforce confirmed REDD+ buyer (overall REDD retirements 107k tCO2e in 2024, 75% drop YoY across all projects). Engie among VCM's largest buyers with 1.7M credits retired (AlliedOffsets 2024 report). Subject of Mongabay/AP investigations on indigenous land-rights and additionality.

projects.name: Southern Cardamom REDD+ (Cambodia)
projects.scandal_notes: Suspended by Verra in 2023 amid investigations into human-rights/forced-evictions allegations; reinstated September 2024 after additional safeguards. Co-developed by Wildlife Alliance and the Cambodian government.
